AC.1.001: Limit information system access to authorized users
AC.1.002: Limit information system access to types of transactions authorized users permitted to execute
AC.2.005: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with CUI rules
AC.2.006: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems
AC.2.007: Employ the principle of least privilege
AC.2.008: Use non-privileged accounts when accessing non-security functions
AC.2.009: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions
AC.2.010: Use session lock after period of inactivity
AC.3.012: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption
AC.3.014: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect confidentiality of remote access sessions
AC.3.017: Separate duties of individuals to reduce risk of malevolent activity
AC.3.018: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions
AC.3.021: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands
AC.4.023: Control CUI that flows between domains
AC.4.025: Periodically review and update CUI program
AU.2.041: Ensure actions of individual users can be traced
AU.2.042: Create and retain system audit logs
AU.3.046: Alert in event of audit logging process failure
AU.3.048: Collect audit information into one or more central repositories
AU.3.050: Limit management of audit logging to subset of privileged users
AU.3.051: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting
AU.3.052: Provide system capability that compares audit records from multiple sources
AU.4.053: Automate analysis of audit logs to identify anomalies
AU.4.054: Review audit logs for broad activity
CM.2.062: Establish and enforce security configuration settings
CM.2.064: Establish and enforce usage restrictions for publicly accessible systems
CM.3.068: Restrict, disable, or prevent use of nonessential programs
CM.3.069: Apply deny-by-exception policy to prevent use of unauthorized software
IA.1.076: Identify information system users, processes, and devices
IA.2.079: Prohibit password reuse for specified generations
IA.2.080: Allow temporary passwords with immediate change requirement
IA.2.081: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords
IA.3.083: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access
IR.3.098: Test the organizational incident response capability
MA.2.112: Provide controls on the tools and personnel performing maintenance
MA.2.113: Ensure equipment removed for maintenance is sanitized
MA.2.114: Check media containing diagnostic programs for malicious code
MA.3.115: Require MFA for remote maintenance sessions
MA.3.116: Supervise maintenance activities of personnel without required access
MP.2.119: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users
MP.2.120: Sanitize or destroy system media before disposal or reuse
MP.3.122: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations
MP.3.123: Prohibit use of portable storage without identifiable owner
PE.1.131: Limit physical access to systems to authorized individuals
PE.1.132: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity

PE.1.134: Control and manage physical access devices
PE.2.135: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure
PS.2.127: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access
RA.3.147: Periodically assess risk to operations and assets
RA.3.148: Scan for vulnerabilities in systems periodically
RM.4.148: Develop and implement risk mitigation plans
SC.1.175: Monitor, control, and protect communications at external boundaries
SC.1.176: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components

SC.3.178: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices
SC.3.179: Control and monitor use of mobile code
SC.3.180: Control and monitor use of VoIP technologies
SC.3.181: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions
SC.3.183: Control and monitor communications at system boundaries

SC.3.186: Implement Domain Name System filtering services
SC.3.188: Implement architectural designs for information security
SC.3.189: Protect the authenticity and integrity of security communications
SC.3.190: Control use of removable media on system components
SC.3.191: Protect portable storage devices containing CUI
SC.3.192: Implement honeypots to detect adversarial activity

SI.1.211: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations
SI.1.213: Perform periodic scans of the information system

SI.2.217: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems
